observations.recommendation: a) Reassess and reconsider the internal controls, policies and procedures adopted by the Municipality in recognizing, monitoring, safeguarding and reporting of its Property, Plant and Equipment: b) Intensify the institutionalization of an Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical inventory of its PPE annually, taking into consideration the required standards and procedures in the Planning, Execution, and Reconciliation of GSO and Accounting records; c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPE ledger cards and the GSO of its property cards as control records to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records and investigate any discrepancy noted and abide with the provisions as prescribed in the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs; d) Ensure that all insurable physical assets are adequately and reliably protected against any damage to or loss of properties pursuant to Republic Act No. 656; e) Facilitate the preparation of documentary requirements to secure the Transfer Certificates of Title (TCT) under the name of the Municipality of Tulunan, Cotabato for all parcels of land to support claims of ownership and to avoid loss of properties in case other interested parties may claim the same in the future; and trace its records/documents to establish ownership of the parcels of land with incomplete data as to acquisition date and cost and if found to be legally owned by the Municipality, expedite the application for the land titles under the name of the Municipality of Tulunan, Cotabato; and f) Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize all completed Road Network Assets on cost or fair value whichever is applicable and direct the Municipal Accountant, the MGSO, the Municipal Engineer and the Inventory Committee to strictly perform their duties and responsibilities in the accounting and reporting of Local Road Network as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: AAR - 2020 Count in September, 2021 - Reconciled road network record with the Office of Engineering the listing provided by the Office of Property, Plant and Equipment: Inventory Committee - Road Network record with the Office of Engineering and Accounting records;
observations.reason: The count took almost four months to complete due to lack of manpower. Some of the PPEs were moved/transferred to and from offices due to some renovations that took place. Road Network listing was emailed on March 2022.

observations.recommendation: a) Require the responsible offices entrusted with inventory items to judiciously maintain stock cards and properly monitor and update the stock cards to generate a more reliable inventory report; b) Ensure that all issuances of inventory items be supported with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) and regularly submit to the Municipal Accountant within five days after end of the month pursuant to Section 189 of the Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units and require the concerned offices to submit liquidation documents to support the issuance on distribution of inventory items; c) Necessitate the Municipal Accountant to institute necessary adjustments in relation to the Inventory account and maintain inventory ledger cards for separate inventory items and promptly update the ledger cards as part of its internal control and timely reconciliation; and d) Review and reassess the accounting policy and procedure of the LGU for inventory undertakings pursuant to Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and other related laws, rules and regulations taking in to consideration the soundness of the internal control adopted by the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 - Recorded/required liquidation before year-end - Restocking does not fall on a year-end thus could not update the year-end balances - To conduct actual count in almost four months to complete due to lack of manpower. Request for supporting documents to complete the inventories. The MSWD and concerned offices committed to submit liquidation documents.
observations.reason: Restocking does not fall on year-end thus could not update the year-end balances; Absence of supporting documents; Lack of manpower to focus on liquidating the inventories.

observations.recommendation: a) Direct the concerned personnel to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advance and failure to comply will constitute a valid cause for the withholding of her salary and the instruction of other sanctions as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and b) Enforce strictly the provision of Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and require the concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to immediately settle the same.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The Municipal Accountant has issued Demand Letters to the concerned official/s.

observations.recommendation: a) The concerned personnel should always ensure that all the expenses incurred are included in the approved investment plan; b) Prepare and submit additional/updated supplemental investment plan for the programs and projects intended to locally stranded individuals and returning overseas Filipino; and c) Incorporate the updated investment plan to the supplemental budget and submit the said plan to COA; d) Submit justification why the 29 barangays resorted to the giving of financial assistance in the form of cash to the barangays; e) Require the 29 Barangays to submit complete documents in support to the liquidation of the assistance given to them; f) Before executing a MOA, ensure that terms and conditions are authorized and allowed under existing laws, rules and regulations; g) Ensure that both parties in the MOA comply with the conditions set forth and faithfully perform their duties and responsibilities as stipulated in the agreement; and h) Require the MSWD to submit liquidation reports with attachments to Accounting Office and furnish copy to COA.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 - Comply with the required posting of the supplemental investment plan and supplemental budget - Submit additional documents - Return the duplicated claims to DSWD - The MSWD issued Demand letters to the municipality and returned duplicated claims to DSWD for validation - The MSWD assured that they will submit liquidation documents; however some barangays directly submitted their vouchers to COA and could not provide the supporting documents.
observations.reason: There are far flung barangays that are difficult to access and do not have enough means of transportation to implement/provide food for the frontliners thus unable to complete the supporting documents. Out of 29 barangays, some barangay failed to provide liquidation.

observations.recommendation: a) The LGU shall report to the DSWD about the noted duplication of cash beneficiaries for further validation/investigation and appropriate action; b) The DSWD shall strictly intensify the validation process before and after the payout to avoid double claims or same scenario to happen in the future. Ensure that no payout shall be allowed, unless complete validation process that consists of eligibility and duplication checks are performed by them and by the LGU and other concerned agencies; and c) Compel the duplicate beneficiaries to return the excess SAP benefits to the government; Disqualify them from receiving the next tranche of the cash assistance as emphasized in Item VIII B. (6.2) of DSWD Memo Circular No. 09 series of 2020.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 - MSWDO issued Demand letters to those who received the assistance twice. - Return the duplicated claims to DSWD for further validation. - The MSWDO coordinated with DSWD for validation and possible sanctions.
observations.reason: Duplication of claims by same beneficiaries; exposure of government funds to possible abuse; need for stricter validation and follow-up actions.

observations.recommendation: a) Ensure that the allotted budget for the Local DRRM Fund are properly appropriated as well as the unutilized balance is judiciously transferred to the special trust fund intended for LDRRM activities with its corresponding cash balance for the year to ascertain that all programs, projects and activities identified in the Local DRRM Fund will be realized accordingly and reported appropriately; b) Properly implement the Local DRRM Plan and optimize the utilization of its Local DRRM Fund to lessen the socioeconomic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community and disaster risks, and to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the community.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 - Transfer of unspent balance to Special Trust Account - Regularly monitor the PPAs to ensure implementati on - Attributed PPAs to address the GAP on the project implementation and monitoring - Provision of budget for prior years Investment Plan - The MLGU and MDRRMO coordinated to address gaps and monitor projects.
observations.reason: Gap between MDRRMO, and MEO on late/unapproval of Investment Plan for prior years; ongoing monitoring of PPAs; some unspent balances pending transfer.

observations.recommendation: a) Maximize the utilization of the 20% development fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of the social, economic and environmental development and optimally utilize the 20% development fund to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; b) Appropriate 20% development fund exclusively to programs, projects and activities which are in the nature of investment or capital expenditures in accordance with the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-01, unless there were other provisional guidelines that would supplement the laws, rules and regulations previously issued; and c) Ascertain that programs, projects and activities should be specifically identified, quantified, monitored, accomplished and reported in accordance with plans and targets set for the year.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 - Conducted multiple PPAs assessment pending - Management tried to implement all its projects. - The MLGU prioritized implementation of continuing programs from previous years.
observations.reason: Low utilization rate since management prioritized continuing programs from previous years; delays in implementation and monitoring; need to assign timelines and follow-up to implement pending projects.

observations.recommendation: a) Strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely submission of the said reports to the COA audit team; b) Require the Heads of implementing departments/offices to ensure that GAD projects, activities and programs are implemented as planned to attain the GAD objectives; c) Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided; d) Require the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; and e) Strengthen the GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 - Attributed PPAs to address the GAP on the GAD. - Provision of trainings and programs to enhance GFPS capabilities. - Committed to maximize utilization and strengthen monitoring.
observations.reason: Need for continued education and capacity building of GFPS; requirement to institutionalize GAD database and improve M&E; some GAD PPAs not implemented due to pandemic constraints.

observations.recommendation: a) Promptly resolve other issues in the delayed implementation of projects, as much as possible fast track the project execution after COVID 19 pandemic if situation warrant, and ensure the proper utilization of funds in accordance with MOA and other pertinent rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 - Provision of support and follow-up to PG for release of funds - Implementation of several projects ongoing and being monitored;
observations.reason: Delayed release of tranches from Provincial Government; issues in implementation and coordination causing slow utilization; ongoing follow-up and monitoring.

observations.recommendation: a) Provide necessary support in enjoining other barangays to establish their own MRF and to promote compliance with segregation; b) Take appropriate action to ensure that the existing MRF of the component barangays has division for each type of wastes and appropriately labeled to guide the residents in the proper segregation of wastes; and c) Include in their plan the long-term management of solid waste, particularly the construction and maintenance of sanitary landfill. This landfill shall be designed, operated and maintained in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned in the said act.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 - Provision of budget for construction of landfill facility - The MLGU provided assistance and coordination to establish sanitary landfill facility. Ongoing construction and compliance activities.
observations.reason: Requirement for long-term planning and funding; ongoing efforts to comply and construct sanitary landfill; continued coordination with barangays required.

observations.recommendation: a) The Local Chief Executive of the MLGU and the PG Head strictly comply with the provisions of the I-Reap Operations Manual, Enterprise Agreement, and COA Circular No. 2007-001 in the implementation of subprojects; b) The concerned officers of the MLGU and PG regularly submit the mandatory reports to avoid the undesirable effects of delayed release of succeeding tranches and late completion of the projects; c) The concerned officer of the MLGU make a regular follow up on the submission of the mandatory reports from the PG; and d) The concerned officers of the MLGU strictly monitor and evaluate the implementation of the project to ensure the completion on the target date and to guarantee the sustainability of the project.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 - Demand for submission of liquidation reports and mandatory reports - Follow up actions to PG for report submissions - Coordination for timely releases and monitoring.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of liquidation reports by the PG; ongoing follow-ups and monitoring by MLGU; some tranches delayed due to late reports.

observations.recommendation: a) Provide provision of budget for project implementation and ensure proper utilization of LDRRMF for planned activities as incorporated in the LDRRMF Investment Plan to increase the level of disaster preparedness and response capability of the Local Government Unit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 - The MLGU tried its best to implement all its projects. Ongoing monitoring and implementation of DRRM projects. Provision of some budgets for continuing programs.
observations.reason: Project implementation affected by resources and prioritization; ongoing efforts to increase accomplishments and implement projects in line with RA 10121.

observations.recommendation: a) Provide budget for the creation of sanitary landfill and to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes and discontinue the use of open dump sites; b) Ensure the landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria provided in RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 - Provision of budget for construction of landfill facility - Ongoing implementation and planning for sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: Ongoing construction of landfill facility; requirement for sustained funding and compliance to RA 9003 design and operational guidelines.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that they will:
a) Reassess and reconsider the internal controls, policies and procedures adopted
by the Municipality in recognizing, monitoring, safeguarding and reporting of
its Property, Plant and Equipment:
b) Intensify the institutionalization of an Inventory Committee to conduct actual
physical inventory of its PPE annually, taking into consideration the required
standards and procedures in Planning, Execution, and Reconciliation of GSO
and Accounting records;
c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPE ledger
cards and the GSO of its property cards as control records to facilitate the
regular reconciliation of records and investigate any discrepancy noted and
abide with the provisions as prescribed in the Government Accounting Manual
for LGUs;
d) Ensure that all insurable physical assets are adequately and reliably protected
against any damage to or loss of properties pursuant to Republic Act No. 656;
e) Facilitate the preparation of documentary requirements to secure the Transfer
Certificates of Title (TCT) under the name of the Municipality of Tulunan,
Cotabato for all parcels of land to support claims of ownership and to avoid loss
of properties in case other interested parties may claim the same in the future;
and trace its records/documents to establish ownership of the parcels of land
with incomplete data as to acquisition date and cost and if found to be legally
owned by the Municipality, expedite the application for the land titles under the
name of the Municipality of Tulunan, Cotabato; and
f) Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize all completed Road Network
Assets on cost or fair value whichever is applicable and direct the Municipal
Accountant, the MGSO, the Municipal Engineer and the Inventory Committee
to strictly perform their duties and responsibilities in the accounting and
reporting of Local Road Network as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-
008.

We recommended and the Management agreed that they will:
a) Require the responsible offices entrusted with inventory items to judiciously
maintain stock cards and properly monitor and update the stock cards to
generate a more reliable inventory report;
b) Ensure that all issuances of inventory items be supported with Requisition and
Issue Slip (RIS) and regularly submit to the Municipal Accountant within five
days after end of the month pursuant to Section 189 of the Government
Accounting Manual for Local Government Units and require the concerned
offices to submit liquidation documents to support the issuance on distribution
of inventory items;
c) Necessitate the Municipal Accountant to institute necessary adjustments in
relation to the Inventory account and maintain inventory ledger cards for
separate inventory items and promptly update the ledger cards as part of its
internal control and timely reconciliation; and
d) Review and reassess the accounting policy and procedure of the LGU for
inventory undertakings pursuant to Government Accounting Manual for LGUs
and other related laws, rules and regulations taking in to consideration the
soundness of the internal control adopted by the LGU.

We recommended and the Management agreed that they will:
a) Direct the concerned personnel to immediately settle the unliquidated cash
advance and failure to comply will constitute a valid cause for the withholding
of her salary and the instruction of other sanctions as provided in COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and
b) Enforce strictly the provision of Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and require the
concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to
immediately settle the same.

We recommended and the Management agreed that they will:
a) Ensure that allotted budget for the Local DRRM Fund are properly
appropriated and the unutilized balance is judiciously transferred to the special
trust fund intended for LDRRM activities with its corresponding cash balance
for the year to ascertain that all programs, projects and activities identified in
the Local DRRM Fund will be realized accordingly and reported appropriately;
and
b) Properly implement the Local DRRM Plan and optimize the utilization of its
Local DRRM Fund to lessen the socioeconomic and environmental impacts of
disasters in the community and disaster risks, and to enhance disaster
preparedness and response capabilities of the community.

We recommended and the Management agreed that they will:
a) Maximize the utilization of the 20% development fund allocated for the
implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned
to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development
and optimally utilize the 20% development fund to help achieve desirable socio-
economic development and environmental outcomes;
b) Appropriate 20% development fund exclusively to programs, projects and
activities which are in the nature of investment or capital expenditures in
accordance with the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-01, unless
there were other provisional guidelines that would supplement the laws, rules
and regulations previously issued; and
c) Ascertain that programs, projects and activities should be specifically
identified, quantified, monitored, accomplished and reported in accordance with
plans and targets set for the year.

We recommended and the Management agreed that they will:
a. Strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014
doe the timely submission of the said reports to the COA audit team;
b. Require the Heads of implementing departments/offices to ensure that GAD
projects, activities and programs are implemented as planned to attain the GAD
objectives;
c) Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-
related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were
provided;
d) Comply with the requirement of Section 37 (C), Rule VI of RA No. 9710 or the
Magna Carta of Women and Section 4.1A of PCW- DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01;
e) Require the GAD Focal Point System to (i) institutionalize the GAD database
with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will
provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy
formulation; (ii) undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender
issues and ensure that the projects, activities and programs are responsive to the
said issues; and (iii) revisit Annex B of PCW- DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for
particular gender issues; and
f) Strengthen the GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under
Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW- DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular
No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to resolve the issue on delayed distribution and ensure the prompt release of the RPT shares to the Barangays on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter in strict compliance with Section 271(d) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to promptly resolve other issues in the delayed implementation of projects, as much as possible fast track the project execution after the COVID 19 pandemic if situation so warrant, and ensure the proper utilization of funds in accordance with MOA and other pertinent rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: Management agreed to promptly resolve other issues in the delayed implementation of projects, as much as possible fast track the project execution after the COVID 19 pandemic if situation so warrant, and ensure the proper utilization of funds in accordance with MOA and other pertinent rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that: a) Provide necessary support in enjoining other barangays to establish their own MRF and to promote compliance with segregation; b) Take appropriate action to ensure that the existing MRF of the component barangays has division for each type of wastes and appropriately labeled to guide the residents in the proper segregation of wastes; and c) Include in their plan the long-term management of solid waste, particularly the construction and maintenance of sanitary landfill. This landfill shall be designed, operated and maintained in accordance with the guidelines/criteria provided in RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that: a) The Local Chief Executive of MLGU and the PG Head strictly comply with the provisions of the I-Reap Operations Manual, Enterprise Investment Agreement, and COA Circular No. 2007-001 in the implementation of subprojects; b) The concerned officers of the MLGU and PG regularly submit the mandatory reports to avoid the undesirable effects of delayed release of succeeding tranches and late completion of the projects; c) The concerned officer of the MLGU make a regular follow up on the submission of the mandatory reports from the PG; and d) The concerned officers of the MLGU strictly monitor and evaluate the implementation of the project to ensure the completion on the target date and to guarantee the sustainability of the project.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: The MLGU has to regularly follow up the submission of reports from the Proponent Group (PG).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the MPDO comply with the provisions of Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 and ensure regular submission of the list of projects at the beginning of the year in proper format as well as the required information of PPAs of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: The Management complied and ensured regular submission of the list of projects at the beginning of the year in proper format as well as the required information of PPAs of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure the implementation of the planned activities and projects as incorporated in the LDRRMF Investment Plan to increase the level of disaster preparedness and response capability of the Local Government Unit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018
observations.reason: The Management tried its best to implement projects. The Management is enjoined to strategize so as to maximize utilization of its resources and attain efficient project implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management identify the LDRRM Investment Plan compliant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 containing a complete functional classification, list of specific projects and activities with sufficient details of funding whether current year appropriation, continuing appropriation or special trust funds from unexpended DRRM Funds of prior years, copy furnish the COA Audit Team. Likewise require the LDRRM Officer to submit monthly the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: (a) maximize its authority and power to collect delinquent taxes and other charges; and (b) intensify its strategies and/or utilize other workable and feasible collection approaches to prevent accumulation of past due accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure that the 20% development fund be fully utilized to attain its desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes in the implementation of development projects.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government adopt a system of payroll payment through the Automated Teller Machines.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) Direct the Supply Officer to properly accomplish the required Purchase Order in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996; and (b) Employ means to ensure timely submission to COA of the POs or any contract entered into by the Municipality, not consistent with COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management identify possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes and discontinue the use of open dump sites. This landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned in the said act.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016
observations.reason: Provision of budget for construction of landfill facility.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, the MGSO, the Municipal Engineer and the Inventory Committee shall strictly perform their duties and responsibilities in the accounting and reporting of Local Road Network as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Not yet complied.
observations.reason: Procedure quite complicated. The MEO could not account all the local roads in the Municipality. Reiterated in Finding No. 2 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Agency Head instruct the Municipal Accountant
and the General Services Officer to expedite the reconciliation between their
records and ascertain the assurance embodied in the PPE accounts specifically the
accuracy, existence and completeness of the government properties; evaluate the
causes of the discrepancies between records and employ necessary internal control
procedures to regulate the transactions pertaining to PPE accounts; and regularly
submit to the Auditor the RCPPE prepared by the Inventory Committee and duly
reconciled with the records of the Accounting and the GSO not later than January
31 of the ensuing year.

Evaluate possible application for insurance of the major insurable
properties of the LGU to ensure that they are adequately and reliably protected
against any damage to or loss of properties pursuant to Republic Act No. 656.

Require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with other offices
concerned, to facilitate the valuation and recording of the five (5) parcel of land
that were already titled in the name of the LGU but remained unrecorded in its
books of accounts to-date.

Also, we recommended that Management facilitate the preparation of
documentary requirements to secure the Transfer Certificates of Title (TCT) under
the name of the Municipality of Tulunan, Cotabato to fully establish claims of
ownership over the said parcels of land and to avoid loss of properties in case other
interested parties may claim the same in the future.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
recognize all completed Road Network Assets on cost or fair value whichever is
applicable and provide the appropriate depreciation for the depreciable component
of the road network following the straight line method without provision for
residual value; and
We further recommended that Management direct the Municipal
Accountant, the MGSO, the Municipal Engineer and the Inventory Committee to
strictly perform their duties and responsibilities in the accounting and reporting of
Local Road Network as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
scrutinize the details of the transactions pertaining to inter-fund transfers,
reconcile the reciprocal accounts on all funds maintained by the Municipality,
prepare reconciliation statement, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct
account balances, thus, present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial
condition as of a given period of time. Reconciliation shall be made regularly to
avoid the accumulation of unreconciled balances of the accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to resolve the issue on delayed
distribution and ensure the prompt release of the RPT shares to the Barangays on a
quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter in strict
compliance with Section 271(d) of RA 7160.

We recommended that Management promptly resolve other issues
in the delayed implementation of projects, as much as possible fast track the
project execution after the COVID 19 pandemic if situation so warrant, and ensure
the proper utilization of funds in accordance with MOA and other pertinent rules
and regulations.

We recommended that Management:
a. Provide necessary support in enjoining other barangays to establish their own
MRF and to promote compliance with segregation;
b. Take appropriate action to ensure that the existing MRF of the component
barangays has division for each type of wastes and appropriately labeled to
guide the residents in the proper segregation of wastes; and
c. Include in their plan the long-term management of solid waste, particularly the
construction and maintenance of sanitary landfill. This landfill shall be
designed, operated and maintained in accordance with the guidelines/criteria
provided in RA 9003.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
a) The Local Chief Executive of MLGU and the PG Head strictly comply with the
provisions of the I-Reap Operations Manual, Enterprise Investment Agreement,
and COA Circular No. 2007-001 in the implementation of subprojects;
b) The concerned officers of the MLGU and PG regularly submit the mandatory
reports to avoid the undesirable effects of delayed release of succeeding
tranches and late completion of the projects;
c) The concerned officer of the MLGU make a regular follow up on the submission
of the mandatory reports from the PG; and
d) The concerned officers of the MLGU strictly monitor and evaluate the
implementation of the project to ensure the completion on the target date and to
guarantee the sustainability of the project.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the MPDO comply with
the provisions of Circular No. 2013-004 and ensure regular submission of the list of
projects at the beginning of the year in proper format as well as the required
quarterly information of PPAs of the municipality.

We further recommended that they must equip themselves on the updates
on government policies, laws, rules and regulations with regards to transparency and
accountability on reporting and monitoring of Projects, Programs and
Activities.

We recommended that Management: a. Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust all book reconciling items and to see to it that all succeeding cash transactions be properly recorded in the books of accounts of the Municipality to come up with a reliable balance of the accounts in the financial statements; furnish the bank immediately of the Bank Reconciliation with specific items for adjustment with the bank; coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and perform a regular reconciliation of the accounts to avoid the accumulation of unreconciled items in the books; and b. Require the concerned personnel to undertake the proper accounting and disposition of stale checks as prescribed in Section 59 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Make Other representation with the PRDP concerned personnel to resolve any issue with regard to the paid mobilization fee and reconcile the records of the Municipality with the latter; and b. Gather and evaluate the reports needed for the liquidation of the Advances to Contractor and related accounts to come up with a fair presentation of the accounts in the books of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Temporarily transferred to Deferred Credits of the Ombudsman
observations.reason: Management is waiting for the final resolution from the Office of the Ombudsman

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to properly evaluate the succeeding appropriations for each concerned office to correctly appropriate each expenditure account under the most appropriate department of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: ensure the implementation of the planned activities and projects as incorporated in the LDRRMF Investment Plan to increase the level of accomplishments in disaster preparedness and response capability of the Local Government Unit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Management tried its best to implement projects.
observations.reason: Management is enjoined to strategize so as to maximize utilization of its resources and attain efficient implementation of the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management identify a comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) compliant to COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 containing a complete functional classification, list of specific projects and activities with sufficient details of funding whether current year appropriation, continuing appropriation or special trust funds from unexpended DRRM Funds of prior years, copy furnish the COA Audit Team. Likewise require the LDRRM Officer to submit monthly the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Complied but some were submitted in delay.
observations.reason: Report on Sources & Utilization of DRRM Fund not submitted monthly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and MPDO comply with the provisions of Circular No. 2013-004 and ensure regular submission of the list of projects at the beginning of the year in proper format as well as the required quarterly information of PPAs of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Complied but some were submitted in delay.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 8 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management expedite the processing and evaluation of the reports needed for the liquidation of the Advances to Contractor account to come up with a reliable data of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 The documents for final resolution were already prepared however, not yet liquidated in the books of the MLGU.
observations.reason: Waiting for the final resolution from the Office of the Ombudsman.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management make a follow-up on the processing of the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties against third party claims and in compliance with Section 449 of the GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Titled in the name of the Municipality of Tulunan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: (a) maximize its authority and power to collect delinquent taxes and other charges; and (b) intensify its law strategies and/or utilize other workable and feasible collection approaches to prevent the accumulation of past due accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 The LGU exerted more effort to collect the delinquency.
observations.reason: Management may adopt other strategies provided by law to improve collection of delinquent Real Property Taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure the 20% development fund be fully utilized to attain said desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes in the implementation of development projects. Adequate lead time preparatory to commencement of procurement process should be in-placed to avoid delays.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Management agreed to ensure the implementation of the said PPAs.
observations.reason: Management is enjoined to strengthen its implementation within the time frame as provided in its AIP. Adequate lead time preparatory to procurement process should be in-placed to avoid delays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adopt a DBP system of payroll payment through the Automated Teller Machines (ATMs), thus, protect the government resources and adopt a more advantageous and efficient payment scheme.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Made a follow-up to the request made by the management.
observations.reason: Considering the time elapsed since the opening of the request made, management is enjoined to closely follow up with the status of the request.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) Direct the Supply Officer to properly accomplish the required Purchase Order in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996; and (b) Employ means to ensure timely submission to COA of the POs or any contract entered into by the Municipality, disregarding not consistent with COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Not all were complied on the contracts were prescribed not submitted with the required period. Management is reminded of sanctions that may be imposed for continuously disregarding proper adherence to government regulations.
observations.reason: Some POs and government contracts were not completely supported with the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management create an Inventory Committee to conduct the mandatory annual physical count of PPE and prepare the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to the General Services Office as a basis for reconciliation. Also, instruct the GSO and the Accountant to reconcile the records of the physical count and appropriate adjustments should be made to account for the obsolete, unserviceable and lost properties.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Conducted a physical count.
observations.reason: The reconciliation of records between the Accounting Office and the General Services Office was not adequately done. Also, Incorporated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) Ensure completeness of the supporting documents of all contracts submitted for review pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001; and (b) Require the personnel in-charge in the submission of the contracts and its supporting documents to maintain a checklist of required documents for each of the transactions to serve as a guide to ensure the completeness of the documents submitted to COA for review.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Submitted documents to COA for review. Some documents were prepared.
observations.reason: Some documents were not completely supported with the required documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management identify possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes and discontinue the use of open dump sites. This landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned in the said act.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Provision of budget for sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: Sanitary landfill not yet established.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management expedite the processing and resolution of the reports needed for the liquidation of the Advances to Contractor account to come up with a reliable data of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Not Complied
observations.reason:
-Waiting for the final evaluation from the Office of the Ombudsman.
-Incorporated in Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that AAR mgmt. (a) require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list of the amount due and collectible for the year; and (b) require the Accountant to record the receivables for the year at the beginning of each year based on the list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer to come up with a reliable presentation of the receivables and corresponding deferred accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Complied

observations.recommendation: We recommend that mgmt. disburse project funds in accordance to the provisions of R.A. 9184 and PRDP Operations Manual Guidelines and appropriate rules of procedure should be strictly followed to avoid the incurrence of irregular expenditure.
observations.managementAction: Complied

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe the perpetual inventory method in accounting for inventories as provided under the NGAS Manual. It is also advisable to have a stockroom provided for inventory items of the agency to protect them from spillage/spoilage and unauthorized use.
observations.managementAction: AAR Supplies were already booked as inventories as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management make a follow-up on the processing of the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties against third party claims and in compliance with Section 449 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Titling in process.
observations.reason: Management is on the process of getting the land transferred in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties against third party claims and in compliance with Section 449 of the GAAM, Volume I.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that mgmt.: (a) maximize from its authority and power to collect delinquent taxes and other charges; and (b) intensify its strategies and/or utilize other workable and feasible collection approaches to prevent the accumulation of past due accounts.
observations.managementAction: Demands were made to delinquent taxpayers.
observations.reason: Delinquent taxes were not fully collected. Some were collected in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that mgmt. expedite the implementation of the said PPAs to attain its desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes in the implementation of development projects.
observations.managementAction: mgmt. agreed to ensure that the 20% development fund be fully utilized to attain its desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes in the implementation of development projects.
observations.reason: Some projects were still ongoing or to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that mgmt. discontinue the practice of allowing deductions on monthly loan amortizations of government/private lending institutions from the personnel's payroll other than those mandated by law. These personnel who contracted loans from these lending establishments must directly remit their payments to the collecting agents/office of these creditors. Moreover, we also recommend that management strictly comply with the provisions of GAA with regard to the minimum take-home pay which is P5,000.00 effective CY 2018.
observations.managementAction: Complied

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Government adopt a system of payroll payment through the Automated Teller Machines.
observations.managementAction: Already submitted requirements to the bank for the adoption of the payroll system.
observations.reason: Payrolls were still on envelope method, although arrangement was already made with the bank for the adoption of the payroll system.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Health Office coordinate with the barangay health centers and determine their needs that can be funded by the PFPR so that the government goals of providing preventive care services to indigents will be fully implemented and thus, best results could be achieved in the most opportune time. Also, maximize the use of the funds intended especially for Drugs and Medicines so that beneficiaries can avail and use of the program.
observations.managementAction: Coordinate with the barangay health centers and determine their needs that can be funded by the PFPR so that the government goals of providing preventive care services to indigents will be fully implemented.
observations.reason: Funds already utilized.

observations.recommendation: Management: (a) Direct the Supply Officer to properly accomplish the required Purchase Order in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996; and (b) Employ means to ensure timely submission to COA of the POs or any contract entered into by the Municipality, not consistent with COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Some were complied on prescribed period.
observations.reason: Some POs and government contracts were not completely supported with the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management create an Inventory Committee to conduct the mandatory annual physical count of PPE and prepare and submit to the Accountant the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
observations.managementAction: Created Inventory Committee to conduct the mandatory annual physical count of PPE and submit the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Accountant.
observations.reason: Conducted incomplete physical count.

observations.recommendation: Mgmt.: (a) Ensure completeness of the supporting documents of all contracts submitted for review and (b) Require the personnel in-charge in the submission of the contracts and its supporting documents to maintain a checklist of required documents for each of the transactions to serve as a guide to ensure the completeness of the documents submitted to COA for review.
observations.managementAction: Assigned staff to monitor the contracts submitted for review and require the personnel in-charge to maintain checklist of required documents.
observations.reason: Some government contracts submitted were not completely supported with the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management identify possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes and discontinue the use of open dump sites. This landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned in the said act.
observations.managementAction: Provision of budget for the creation of sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: The LGU is using MRF (materials recovery facility) to collect the municipality’s wastes and garbage.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management (a) require the Municipal Treasurer to
furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list of the amount due and
collectible for the year; and (b) require the Accountant to record the receivables for
the year at the beginning of each year based on the list submitted by the Municipal
Treasurer to come up with a reliable data of the said receivables and corresponding
deferred accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that management disburse project funds in accordance to
the provisions of R.A. 9184 and PRDP Operations Manual Guidelines and
appropriate rules of procedure should be strictly followed to avoid the incurrence
of irregular expenditure.

We recommended that management expedite the processing and evaluation
of the reports needed for the liquidation of the Advances to Contractor account to
come up with a reliable data of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and management agreed to strictly observe the perpetual
inventory method in accounting for inventories as provided under the NGAS
Manual. It is also advisable to have a stockroom provided for the inventory items
of the agency to protect them from spillage/spoilage and unauthorized use.

We recommended that management make a follow-up on the processing of
the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the
properties against third party claims and in compliance with Section 449 of the
GAAM, Volume I.

We recommended and management agreed to: (a) maximize its authority
and power to collect delinquent taxes and other charges; and (b) intensify its
strategies and/or utilize other workable and feasible collection approaches to
prevent the accumulation of past due accounts.

We recommended and management agreed to ensure that the 20%
development fund be fully utilized to attain its desirable socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes in the implementation of
development projects.

We recommended that management discontinue the practice of allowing
deductions on monthly loan amortizations of government/private lending
institutions from the personnel's payroll other than those mandated by law. These
personnel who contracted loans from these lending establishments must directly
remit their payments to the collecting agents/office of these creditors. Moreover,
we also recommend that management strictly comply with the provisions of GAA
with regard to the minimum take-home pay which is P5,000.00 effective CY 2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Government adopt a system of payroll
payment through the Automated Teller Machines.

furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list of the amount due and
collectible for the year; and (b) require the Accountant to record the receivables for

of irregular expenditure.

of the reports needed for the liquidation of the Advances to Contractor account to

inventory method in accounting for inventories as provided under the NGAS

of the agency to protect them from spillage/spoilage and unauthorized use.

the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the

deductions on monthly loan amortizations of government/private lending

We recommend that the Municipal Health Office coordinate with the barangay health centers and determine their needs that can be funded by the PFPR so that the government goals of providing preventive care services to indigents will be fully implemented and thus, best results could be achieved in the most opportune time. Also, need to pre-canvass the cost of medicines and adjust the PR based on the prevailing prices.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2017, management was able to coordinate with the barangay health centers and purchase medicines charged to PFPR totaling P1,466,312.90.
observations.reason: Management were able to coordinate and procure medicines; further action needed to fully utilize the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prepare and require the Municipal’s Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) to be approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: Management continue to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report and submit the same every end of the semester.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: (a) Direct the Supply Officer to properly accomplish the required Purchase Order in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996; and (b) Employ means to ensure timely submission of the POs and attached contracts and supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the POs and other contracts with attached documents. Only those of material value were submitted on time due to the distance from the LGU to COA office.
observations.reason: There were still delay in the submission of contracts and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management create an Inventory Committee to conduct the mandatory annual physical count of PPE and prepare and submit to the Accountant the Inventory Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) as a basis for reconciliation. Also, instruct the GSO and the Accountant to reconcile the records of physical count and appropriate adjustments should be made to account for the obsolete, unserviceable and lost properties.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2017 management created an inventory team and the latter had conducted the mandatory annual inventory and submitted the inventory report.
observations.reason: However, the inventory count report is still for reconciliation with the accounting’s PPE schedule; lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: a. Ensure to focus on the completeness of the supporting documents of all contracts submitted for review pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated Feb. 12, 2009. b. Require the personnel in-charge in the submission of the contracts and its supporting documents to maintain a checklist of required documents for each of the transactions to serve as a guide to ensure the completeness of the documents submitted to COA for review.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the contracts with attached documents. Management hired a dedicated staff and enhanced the tabbing of documents.
observations.reason: Only those of material value were submitted on time due to distance from the LGU to COA office. There were still delays; enhanced tabbing and checklists implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Require the Municipal Accounting Office to record the purchases of these drugs and medicines and laboratory supplies as inventory initially under inventory account; b. Ensure that the Municipal Health Office maintains stock cards to upkeep the records of drugs, medicines and laboratory fund to accounting office.
observations.managementAction: Not complied. The MHO need manpower to monitor the inventory. To be implemented starting CY 2018. The MHO need to submit report of medicine utilization per fund to accounting.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to monitor the inventory; MHO to submit reports and maintain stock cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive see to it that the 20% EDF be optimally utilized to help attain the socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. Also, strictly comply with the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Some projects were fully implemented in CY 2017. The LGU started to implement some of the projects in the last quarter of 2017. There were projects ongoing or for implementation; some were completed but not yet billed.
observations.reason: Some projects implemented; others still ongoing or for reprogramming; delays caused by timing and billing.

observations.recommendation: Enjoin the LGU to identify possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for waste materials and discontinue the use of open dump sites. Provide budget and design the sanitary landfill in accordance with the requirements.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is still using the open dump site for final disposal of waste materials. However, the establishment of sanitary landfill is on process.
observations.reason: Provision of budget in 2018; establishment process ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Enjoin the General Services Office to regularly monitor the expiration of all motor vehicles registration and its renewal/registration to avoid invalidation and fines/penalties for late/non-registration.
observations.managementAction: With latest LTO registration of vehicles and the GSO conducts regular monitoring of motor vehicle registration.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management resort to National Food Authority as primary supplier or source of rice in all its procurement whenever it grants rice in connection with the management functions or aid to its constituents. Also, forge an appropriate Memorandum of Agreement to facilitate and operationalize the rice procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Procured rice from NFA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Health Office determine their PFPR needs that can be funded by the PFPR so that the government goals of providing preventive care services to indigents will be achieved in the most opportune time. Also, need to pre- canvass the cost of medicines.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2017, management were able to coordinate with the barangay health centers and charged to PFPR totaling P1,466,312.90. Management will continue to procure medicines in the ensuing year to fully utilized the fund.
observations.reason: need to pre- canvass the cost of medicines based on the current prices. maximize the use of the funds intended especially for Drugs and Medicines so that beneficiaries can avail and use of the program.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management continue to prepare and submit the Government Procurement and Monitoring Report to GPPB.
observations.managementAction: Require the Municipal’s Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Government Procurement and Monitoring Report to be approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: (a) Direct the Supply Officer to properly accomplish the required documents of all the transactions to accomplish the Purchase Order in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996; and (b) Employ means to ensure timely submission of the POs and contracts and supporting documents to COA.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the POs and other contracts with attached supporting documents. Only those of material value were submitted on time due to the distance from the LGU to COA office. Enhanced the tabbing of documents.
observations.reason: There were still delay in the submission of contracts, POs and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management create an Inventory Committee to conduct the mandatory annual physical count of PPE and prepare and submit to the Accountant the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) as a basis for reconciliation. Also, instruct the GSO and the Accountant to reconcile the records of physical count and appropriate adjustments should be made to account for the obsolete, unserviceable and lost properties.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2017 created an inventory team and the latter had conducted mandatory annual inventory and submitted the inventory report of physical count of PPE. However, the inventory count report is still for reconciliation with the accounting’s PPE schedule.
observations.reason: There was lack of manpower; reconciliation with the accounting records is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: a. Ensure to focus on the completeness of the supporting documents of all contract documents and b. Require the personnel in-charge in the submission of the contracts and its supporting documents to maintain a checklist of required documents for each of the transactions to serve as a guide to ensure the completeness of the documents submitted to COA for review.
observations.managementAction: The LGU hired a dedicated staff to focus on the submission of the contracts. Enhanced the tabbing of documents and require the personnel in-charge to maintain a checklist of required documents for each transaction.
observations.reason: Only those of material value were submitted on time due to the distance from the LGU to COA office. There were still delays in submission of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Require the Municipal Accounting Office to record the purchases of these drugs and medicines and laboratory supplies initially under inventory account; and b. Ensure that the Municipal Health Office maintains stock cards to upkeep the records of drugs, medicines and laboratory fund to accounting office.
observations.managementAction: Not complied. The MHO need to submit report of medicine utilization per laboratory fund to accounting office. The MHO to maintain stock cards to upkeep the records of drugs, medicines and laboratory supplies.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to monitor the inventory. The MHO needs to submit report of medicine utilization per laboratory fund to accounting office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive see to it that the 20% EDF be optimally utilized to help attain the socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. Also, strictly comply with the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Some projects were fully implemented in CY 2017. There were some projects ongoing or partially implemented and some for reprogramming. Management started to implement some of the projects in the last quarter of CY 2017 and some were completed but not yet billed.
observations.reason: Some projects were still for implementation or reprogramming; some completed but not yet billed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management identify possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for waste materials and discontinue the use of open dump sites in accordance with RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is still using the open dump site for final disposal of waste materials. However, the landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned in the said act. Provision of budget in 2018.
observations.reason: This landfill establishment and process is ongoing. Provision of budget in 2018.

observations.recommendation: Enjoin the General Services Office to regularly monitor the expiration of all motor vehicle registrations and its timely renewal/registration to avoid invalidation and fines/penalties for late/non-registration.
observations.managementAction: With latest LTO registration of vehicles and GSO conducts regular monitoring of motor vehicle registration. The GSO regularly monitors registration and renewal.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management resort to National Food Authority as primary supplier or whenever it grants rice in connection with the management functions or aid to its constituents. Also, forge an appropriate Memorandum of Agreement to facilitate and operationalize the rice procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Procured rice from NFA. The LGU resorted to National Food Authority as source of rice in its procurement whenever it grants rice in connection with the management functions or aid to its constituents. Also, forged appropriate arrangements to facilitate procurement process.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
observations.reason: Lack of personnel. Change administrator. Reorganization of BAC.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management ensure all copies of approved contracts, awarded purchase orders and contracts with their supporting documents be submitted within 5 working days from approval or issuance and within 24 hours after accepting deliveries of procured items for review, evaluation, inspection of items procured by the agency.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
observations.reason: Unavailability of transportation equipment. Reorganization of the BAC and its Secretariat

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management exert their best effort to fully accomplish the targeted priority development projects and programs embodied in the Annual Investment Plan pursuant to its mandate. Strictly administer and supervise the implementation of development projects and likewise monitor its implementation. Determine the reason for non-implementation of some projects and evaluate the possibility to make amendments to some projects which are not feasible for implementation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
observations.reason: Lack of personnel Change administrator Reorganization of BAC

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer to conduct annual physical count of properties and submit to the accountant the Report on the Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) as basis for reconciliation. Also, instruct the Municipal GSO and Accountant to reconcile the records with the physical count and appropriate adjustments should be made to account for unserviceable, obsolete and lost properties.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
observations.reason: Delayed physical inventory count, thus failure to reconciliation of physical count and records before the submission of FS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Health Office coordinate with the barangay
health centers and determine their needs that can be funded by the PFPR so that
the government goals of providing preventive care services to indigents will be
fully implemented and thus, best results could be achieved in the most opportune
time. Also, maximize the use of the funds intended especially for Drugs and
Medicines so that beneficiaries can avail and use of the program.

We recommended that the management will require the Municipals Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be
approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity and submit the same to the
Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic
format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the Supply Officer to properly accomplish the required Purchase
Order in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15,
1996.

b. Employ means to ensure timely submission to COA of the POs or any
contract entered into by the Municipality, consistent with COA Circular
2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended to the management to create an Inventory Committee to
conduct the mandatory annual physical count of PPE and prepare and submit to
the Accountant the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) as a basis for reconciliation. Also, instruct the GSO and the
Accountant to reconcile the records of physical count and appropriate adjustments
should be made to account for the obsolete, unserviceable and lost properties.

We recommended that Management:

a. Ensure completeness of the supporting documents of all contracts
submitted for review pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001 dated Feb.
12, 2009.

b. Require the personnel in-charge in the submission of the contracts
and its supporting documents to maintain a checklist of required
documents for each of the transactions to serve as a guide to ensure
the completeness of the documents submitted to COA for review.

c. Require the Municipal Accounting Office to record the purchases of these
drugs and medicines and laboratory supplies and materials for stock
initially under inventory account;

d. Ensure that the Municipal Health Office maintains stock cards to upkeep
the records of drugs, medicines and laboratory supplies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive see to it that the 20% EDF be
optimally utilized to help attain the socio-economic development and
environmental outcomes. Also, strictly comply with the DILG-DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended to the management to identify possible sanitary landfill to
develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes and discontinue the
use of open dump sites. This landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance
with the guidelines/criteria mentioned in the said act.

Enjoin the General Services Office to regularly monitor the expiration of all motor vehicles registration and its timely renewal/registration to avoid invalidation and fines/penalties for late/non-registration.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
observations.reason: Lack of personnel

observations.recommendation: We recommend: Adhere strictly with the rules and regulations for granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as prescribed under Sec. 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 97-002. Send demand letters to the concerned employees. If no settlement or satisfactory explanation is received within 30 days, cause the withholding of salary due to the accountable officer/s. Refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers who have previous unsettled cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Bids and Awards Committee to submit the needed documentary requirements to support the payment of honoraria as basis in evaluating the propriety and validity of the claim. Stop the processing of claims unless the transaction is supported with proper documentation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local chief executive to secure copies of PNDF Volume 1, current edition, and to direct the Municipal Health Officer and other requisitioning officers to refer as basis for procurements of drugs the PNDF and thereby supplement certification for each request the purchase thereof.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management will resort to National Food Authority as primary supplier or source of rice in all its procurement whenever it grants rice in connection with the management functions or aid to its constituents. Also, forge with the NFA an appropriate Memorandum of Agreement to facilitate and operationalize the rice procurement process.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
observations.reason: There were small purchases that NFA would not cater and the LGU resorted to purchase outside NFA. The 9184 procurement procedure was applied.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management to discontinue the practice of deducting payments on monthly loan amortizations from the personnel payroll. These personnel who contracted loans from these lending establishments must directly remit their payments to the collecting agents/office of these creditors.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
observations.reason: Payroll officer failed to follow the instruction.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendations that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator design the programs, activities and projects more definite, specific and measurable in order that accomplishment of the projects can be evaluated in line with its standards.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
